title: astragalus monspessulanus var. teresianus
intro: Astragalus monspessulanus var. teresianus, also known as the Spurred Vetch, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family, Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula, often used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, providing potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced cardiovascular health. Astragalus monspessulanus var. teresianus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or dried extract, which are commonly used to support immune system function and overall well-being.
